preamble: The elision of [t] is a common lenition process, particularly in word-final positions or in complex consonant clusters where the stop is simplified to increase ease of articulation.

examples.note: Latvian systematically dropped word-final *-t; e.g., PIE nominative *-nt → Latvian zero. Cf. Lithuanian 'ėst' (to eat) vs Latvian 'ēst' (earlier *ēsti → *ēst → ēst with different retention).
Loss of the dental stop in the /st/ cluster.
Complete elision of the intervocalic dental stop.
